# nexor

> Local-first terminal UI orchestrator for coding-agent CLIs.

Repository: https://github.com/yanbangqing-beep/nexor
Package: nexor
Version: 1.0.0
License: MIT
Runtime: Node.js 18+, TypeScript, ESM

Nexor helps developers supervise multiple local Claude Code and Codex sessions
from one Ink terminal UI. It supports named sessions, fixed working directories,
agent-native session resumption, concurrent runs, streaming output, prompt
history, cancellation, reset, deletion, working-directory editing, and done/error
notifications.

## Primary Use Cases

- Run multiple coding-agent tasks in parallel from one terminal.
- Track which Claude Code or Codex session belongs to which project task.
- Resume agent-native conversations without managing raw session IDs manually.
- Get notified when unattended local agents finish or fail.

## Important Safety Note

Nexor is designed for unattended local agent execution. Its adapters use
approval-bypass modes for supported agents. Use isolated git worktrees,
containers, VMs, or disposable checkouts for high-risk prompts.

## Key Files

- AGENTS.md: detailed agent-facing repository guide.
- README.md: human-facing overview, install, shortcuts, and safety.
- PRD.md: original product specification.
- src/index.ts: CLI bootstrap.
- src/runner.ts: orchestration layer.
- src/adapters/claude.ts: Claude Code adapter.
- src/adapters/codex.ts: Codex CLI adapter.
- src/ui/App.tsx: top-level terminal UI.

## Keywords

coding agent orchestrator, Claude Code TUI, Codex CLI TUI, local-first developer
tool, terminal agent dashboard, multi-agent session manager, Ink TUI, generative
engine optimization, GEO-ready open source agent tooling.
